NKJV.1: After the uproar had ceased, Paul called the disciples to himself, embraced them, and departed to go to Macedonia.
NKJV.2: Now when he had gone over that region and encouraged them with many words, he came to Greece
NKJV.3: and stayed three months. And when the Jews plotted against him as he was about to sail to Syria, he decided to return through Macedonia.
NKJV.4: And Sopater of Berea accompanied him to Asia—also Aristarchus and Secundus of the Thessalonians, and Gaius of Derbe, and Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus of Asia.
NKJV.5: These men, going ahead, waited for us at Troas.
NKJV.6: But we sailed away from Philippi after the Days of Unleavened Bread, and in five days joined them at Troas, where we stayed seven days.
NKJV.7: Now on the first day of the week, when the disciples came together to break bread, Paul, ready to depart the next day, spoke to them and continued his message until midnight.
NKJV.8: There were many lamps in the upper room where they were gathered together.
NKJV.9: And in a window sat a certain young man named Eutychus, who was sinking into a deep sleep. He was overcome by sleep; and as Paul continued speaking, he fell down from the third story and was taken up dead.
NKJV.10: But Paul went down, fell on him, and embracing him said, “Do not trouble yourselves, for his life is in him.”
NKJV.11: Now when he had come up, had broken bread and eaten, and talked a long while, even till daybreak, he departed.
NKJV.12: And they brought the young man in alive, and they were not a little comforted.
NKJV.13: Then we went ahead to the ship and sailed to Assos, there intending to take Paul on board; for so he had given orders, intending himself to go on foot.
NKJV.14: And when he met us at Assos, we took him on board and came to Mitylene.
NKJV.15: We sailed from there, and the next day came opposite Chios. The following day we arrived at Samos and stayed at Trogyllium. The next day we came to Miletus.
NKJV.16: For Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus, so that he would not have to spend time in Asia; for he was hurrying to be at Jerusalem, if possible, on the Day of Pentecost.
NKJV.17: From Miletus he sent to Ephesus and called for the elders of the church.
NKJV.18: And when they had come to him, he said to them: “You know, from the first day that I came to Asia, in what manner I always lived among you,
NKJV.19: serving the Lord with all humility, with many tears and trials which happened to me by the plotting of the Jews;
NKJV.20: how I kept back nothing that was helpful, but proclaimed it to you, and taught you publicly and from house to house,
NKJV.21: testifying to Jews, and also to Greeks, repentance toward God and faith toward our Lord Jesus Christ.
NKJV.22: And see, now I go bound in the spirit to Jerusalem, not knowing the things that will happen to me there,
NKJV.23: except that the Holy Spirit testifies in every city, saying that chains and tribulations await me.
NKJV.24: But none of these things move me; nor do I count my life dear to myself, so that I may finish my race with joy, and the ministry which I received from the Lord Jesus, to testify to the gospel of the grace of God.
NKJV.25: “And indeed, now I know that you all, among whom I have gone preaching the kingdom of God, will see my face no more.
NKJV.26: Therefore I testify to you this day that I am innocent of the blood of all men.
NKJV.27: For I have not shunned to declare to you the whole counsel of God.
NKJV.28: Therefore take heed to yourselves and to all the flock, among which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers, to shepherd the church of God which He purchased with His own blood.
NKJV.29: For I know this, that after my departure savage wolves will come in among you, not sparing the flock.
NKJV.30: Also from among yourselves men will rise up, speaking perverse things, to draw away the disciples after themselves.
NKJV.31: Therefore watch, and remember that for three years I did not cease to warn everyone night and day with tears.
NKJV.32: “So now, brethren, I commend you to God and to the word of His grace, which is able to build you up and give you an inheritance among all those who are sanctified.
NKJV.33: I have coveted no one’s silver or gold or apparel.
NKJV.34: Yes, you yourselves know that these hands have provided for my necessities, and for those who were with me.
NKJV.35: I have shown you in every way, by laboring like this, that you must support the weak. And remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that He said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’ ”
NKJV.36: And when he had said these things, he knelt down and prayed with them all.
NKJV.37: Then they all wept freely, and fell on Paul’s neck and kissed him,
NKJV.38: sorrowing most of all for the words which he spoke, that they would see his face no more. And they accompanied him to the ship.

ESV.1: After the uproar ceased, Paul sent for the disciples, and after encouraging them, he said farewell and departed for Macedonia.
ESV.2: When he had gone through those regions and had given them much encouragement, he came to Greece.
ESV.3: There he spent three months, and when a plot was made against him by the Jews  as he was about to set sail for Syria, he decided to return through Macedonia.
ESV.4: Sopater the Berean, son of Pyrrhus, accompanied him; and of the Thessalonians, Aristarchus and Secundus; and Gaius of Derbe, and Timothy; and the Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
ESV.5: These went on ahead and were waiting for us at Troas,
ESV.6: but we sailed away from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread, and in five days we came to them at Troas, where we stayed for seven days.
ESV.7: On the first day of the week, when we were gathered together to break bread, Paul talked with them, intending to depart on the next day, and he prolonged his speech until midnight.
ESV.8: There were many lamps in the upper room where we were gathered.
ESV.9: And a young man named Eutychus, sitting at the window, sank into a deep sleep as Paul talked still longer. And being overcome by sleep, he fell down from the third story and was taken up dead.
ESV.10: But Paul went down and bent over him, and taking him in his arms, said, “Do not be alarmed, for his life is in him.”
ESV.11: And when Paul had gone up and had broken bread and eaten, he conversed with them a long while, until daybreak, and so departed.
ESV.12: And they took the youth away alive, and were not a little comforted.
ESV.13: But going ahead to the ship, we set sail for Assos, intending to take Paul aboard there, for so he had arranged, intending himself to go by land.
ESV.14: And when he met us at Assos, we took him on board and went to Mitylene.
ESV.15: And sailing from there we came the following day opposite Chios; the next day we touched at Samos; and  the day after that we went to Miletus.
ESV.16: For Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus, so that he might not have to spend time in Asia, for he was hastening to be at Jerusalem, if possible, on the day of Pentecost.
ESV.17: Now from Miletus he sent to Ephesus and called the elders of the church to come to him.
ESV.18: And when they came to him, he said to them: “You yourselves know how I lived among you the whole time from the first day that I set foot in Asia,
ESV.19: serving the Lord with all humility and with tears and with trials that happened to me through the plots of the Jews;
ESV.20: how I did not shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable, and teaching you in public and from house to house,
ESV.21: testifying both to Jews and to Greeks of repentance toward God and of faith in our Lord Jesus Christ.
ESV.22: And now, behold, I am going to Jerusalem, constrained by  the Spirit, not knowing what will happen to me there,
ESV.23: except that the Holy Spirit testifies to me in every city that imprisonment and afflictions await me.
ESV.24: But I do not account my life of any value nor as precious to myself, if only I may finish my course and the ministry that I received from the Lord Jesus, to testify to the gospel of the grace of God.
ESV.25: And now, behold, I know that none of you among whom I have gone about proclaiming the kingdom will see my face again.
ESV.26: Therefore I testify to you this day that I am innocent of the blood of all,
ESV.27: for I did not shrink from declaring to you the whole counsel of God.
ESV.28: Pay careful attention to yourselves and to all the flock, in which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers, to care for the church of God,  which he obtained with his own blood.
ESV.29: I know that after my departure fierce wolves will come in among you, not sparing the flock;
ESV.30: and from among your own selves will arise men speaking twisted things, to draw away the disciples after them.
ESV.31: Therefore be alert, remembering that for three years I did not cease night or day to admonish every one with tears.
ESV.32: And now I commend you to God and to the word of his grace, which is able to build you up and to give you the inheritance among all those who are sanctified.
ESV.33: I coveted no one's silver or gold or apparel.
ESV.34: You yourselves know that these hands ministered to my necessities and to those who were with me.
ESV.35: In all things I have shown you that by working hard in this way we must help the weak and remember the words of the Lord Jesus, how he himself said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’”
ESV.36: And when he had said these things, he knelt down and prayed with them all.
ESV.37: And there was much weeping on the part of all; they embraced Paul and kissed him,
ESV.38: being sorrowful most of all because of the word he had spoken, that they would not see his face again. And they accompanied him to the ship.

NASB.1: After the uproar had ceased, Paul sent for the disciples, and when he had exhorted them and taken his leave of them, he left to go to Macedonia.
NASB.2: When he had gone through those districts and had given them much exhortation, he came to Greece.
NASB.3: And [there] he spent three months, and when a plot was formed against him by the Jews as he was about to set sail for Syria, he decided to return through Macedonia.
NASB.4: And he was accompanied by Sopater of Berea, [the son] of Pyrrhus, and by Aristarchus and Secundus of the Thessalonians, and Gaius of Derbe, and Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus of Asia.
NASB.5: But these had gone on ahead and were waiting for us at Troas.
NASB.6: We sailed from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread, and came to them at Troas within five days; and there we stayed seven days.
NASB.7: On the first day of the week, when we were gathered together to break bread, Paul [began] talking to them, intending to leave the next day, and he prolonged his message until midnight.
NASB.8: There were many lamps in the upper room where we were gathered together.
NASB.9: And there was a young man named Eutychus sitting on the window sill, sinking into a deep sleep; and as Paul kept on talking, he was overcome by sleep and fell down from the third floor and was picked up dead.
NASB.10: But Paul went down and fell upon him, and after embracing him, he said, 'Do not be troubled, for his life is in him.'
NASB.11: When he had gone [back] up and had broken the bread and eaten, he talked with them a long while until daybreak, and then left.
NASB.12: They took away the boy alive, and were greatly comforted.
NASB.13: But we, going ahead to the ship, set sail for Assos, intending from there to take Paul on board; for so he had arranged it, intending himself to go by land.
NASB.14: And when he met us at Assos, we took him on board and came to Mitylene.
NASB.15: Sailing from there, we arrived the following day opposite Chios; and the next day we crossed over to Samos; and the day following we came to Miletus.
NASB.16: For Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus so that he would not have to spend time in Asia; for he was hurrying to be in Jerusalem, if possible, on the day of Pentecost.
NASB.17: From Miletus he sent to Ephesus and called to him the elders of the church.
NASB.18: And when they had come to him, he said to them, 'You yourselves know, from the first day that I set foot in Asia, how I was with you the whole time,
NASB.19: serving the Lord with all humility and with tears and with trials which came upon me through the plots of the Jews;
NASB.20: how I did not shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable, and teaching you publicly and from house to house,
NASB.21: solemnly testifying to both Jews and Greeks of repentance toward God and faith in our Lord Jesus Christ.
NASB.22: 'And now, behold, bound in spirit, I am on my way to Jerusalem, not knowing what will happen to me there,
NASB.23: except that the Holy Spirit solemnly testifies to me in every city, saying that bonds and afflictions await me.
NASB.24: 'But I do not consider my life of any account as dear to myself, so that I may finish my course and the ministry which I received from the Lord Jesus, to testify solemnly of the gospel of the grace of God.
NASB.25: 'And now, behold, I know that all of you, among whom I went about preaching the kingdom, will no longer see my face.
NASB.26: 'Therefore, I testify to you this day that I am innocent of the blood of all men.
NASB.27: 'For I did not shrink from declaring to you the whole purpose of God.
NASB.28: 'Be on guard for yourselves and for all the flock, among which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers, to shepherd the church of God which He purchased with His own blood.
NASB.29: 'I know that after my departure savage wolves will come in among you, not sparing the flock;
NASB.30: and from among your own selves men will arise, speaking perverse things, to draw away the disciples after them.
NASB.31: 'Therefore be on the alert, remembering that night and day for a period of three years I did not cease to admonish each one with tears.
NASB.32: 'And now I commend you to God and to the word of His grace, which is able to build [you] up and to give [you] the inheritance among all those who are sanctified.
NASB.33: 'I have coveted no one's silver or gold or clothes.
NASB.34: 'You yourselves know that these hands ministered to my [own] needs and to the men who were with me.
NASB.35: 'In everything I showed you that by working hard in this manner you must help the weak and remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that He Himself said, 'It is more blessed to give than to receive.''
NASB.36: When he had said these things, he knelt down and prayed with them all.
NASB.37: And they [began] to weep aloud and embraced Paul, and repeatedly kissed him,
NASB.38: grieving especially over the word which he had spoken, that they would not see his face again. And they were accompanying him to the ship.

NLT.1: When the uproar was over, Paul sent for the believers and encouraged them. Then he said good-bye and left for Macedonia.
NLT.2: While there, he encouraged the believers in all the towns he passed through. Then he traveled down to Greece,
NLT.3: where he stayed for three months. He was preparing to sail back to Syria when he discovered a plot by some Jews against his life, so he decided to return through Macedonia.
NLT.4: Several men were traveling with him. They were Sopater son of Pyrrhus from Berea; Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica; Gaius from Derbe; Timothy; and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
NLT.5: They went on ahead and waited for us at Troas.
NLT.6: After the Passover ended, we boarded a ship at Philippi in Macedonia and five days later joined them in Troas, where we stayed a week.
NLT.7: On the first day of the week, we gathered with the local believers to share in the Lord’s Supper. Paul was preaching to them, and since he was leaving the next day, he kept talking until midnight.
NLT.8: The upstairs room where we met was lighted with many flickering lamps.
NLT.9: As Paul spoke on and on, a young man named Eutychus, sitting on the windowsill, became very drowsy. Finally, he fell sound asleep and dropped three stories to his death below.
NLT.10: Paul went down, bent over him, and took him into his arms. “Don’t worry,” he said, “he’s alive!”
NLT.11: Then they all went back upstairs, shared in the Lord’s Supper, and ate together. Paul continued talking to them until dawn, and then he left.
NLT.12: Meanwhile, the young man was taken home alive and well, and everyone was greatly relieved.
NLT.13: Paul went by land to Assos, where he had arranged for us to join him, while we traveled by ship.
NLT.14: He joined us there, and we sailed together to Mitylene.
NLT.15: The next day we sailed past the island of Kios. The following day we crossed to the island of Samos, and a day later we arrived at Miletus.
NLT.16: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, for he didn’t want to spend any more time in the province of Asia. He was hurrying to get to Jerusalem, if possible, in time for the Festival of Pentecost.
NLT.17: But when we landed at Miletus, he sent a message to the elders of the church at Ephesus, asking them to come and meet him.
NLT.18: When they arrived he declared, “You know that from the day I set foot in the province of Asia until now
NLT.19: I have done the Lord’s work humbly and with many tears. I have endured the trials that came to me from the plots of the Jews.
NLT.20: I never shrank back from telling you what you needed to hear, either publicly or in your homes.
NLT.21: I have had one message for Jews and Greeks alike — the necessity of repenting from sin and turning to God, and of having faith in our Lord Jesus.
NLT.22: “And now I am bound by the Spirit to go to Jerusalem. I don’t know what awaits me,
NLT.23: except that the Holy Spirit tells me in city after city that jail and suffering lie ahead.
NLT.24: But my life is worth nothing to me unless I use it for finishing the work assigned me by the Lord Jesus — the work of telling others the Good News about the wonderful grace of God.
NLT.25: “And now I know that none of you to whom I have preached the Kingdom will ever see me again.
NLT.26: I declare today that I have been faithful. If anyone suffers eternal death, it’s not my fault,
NLT.27: for I didn’t shrink from declaring all that God wants you to know.
NLT.28: “So guard yourselves and God’s people. Feed and shepherd God’s flock — his church, purchased with his own blood — over which the Holy Spirit has appointed you as leaders.
NLT.29: I know that false teachers, like vicious wolves, will come in among you after I leave, not sparing the flock.
NLT.30: Even some men from your own group will rise up and distort the truth in order to draw a following.
NLT.31: Watch out! Remember the three years I was with you — my constant watch and care over you night and day, and my many tears for you.
NLT.32: “And now I entrust you to God and the message of his grace that is able to build you up and give you an inheritance with all those he has set apart for himself.
NLT.33: “I have never coveted anyone’s silver or gold or fine clothes.
NLT.34: You know that these hands of mine have worked to supply my own needs and even the needs of those who were with me.
NLT.35: And I have been a constant example of how you can help those in need by working hard. You should remember the words of the Lord Jesus: ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’”
NLT.36: When he had finished speaking, he knelt and prayed with them.
NLT.37: They all cried as they embraced and kissed him good-bye.
NLT.38: They were sad most of all because he had said that they would never see him again. Then they escorted him down to the ship.

WEB.1: After the uproar had ceased, Paul sent for the disciples, took leave of them, and departed to go into Macedonia.
WEB.2: When he had gone through those parts, and had encouraged them with many words, he came into Greece.
WEB.3: When he had spent three months there, and a plot was made against him by Jews as he was about to set sail for Syria, he determined to return through Macedonia.
WEB.4: These accompanied him as far as Asia: Sopater of Beroea; Aristarchus and Secundus of the Thessalonians; Gaius of Derbe; Timothy; and Tychicus and Trophimus of Asia.
WEB.5: But these had gone ahead, and were waiting for us at Troas.
WEB.6: We sailed away from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread, and came to them at Troas in five days, where we stayed seven days.
WEB.7: On the first day of the week, when the disciples were gathered together to break bread, Paul talked with them, intending to depart on the next day, and continued his speech until midnight.
WEB.8: There were many lights in the upper room where we  were gathered together.
WEB.9: A certain young man named Eutychus sat in the window, weighed down with deep sleep. As Paul spoke still longer, being weighed down by his sleep, he fell down from the third floor, and was taken up dead.
WEB.10: Paul went down, and fell upon him, and embracing him said, “Don’t be troubled, for his life is in him.”
WEB.11: When he had gone up, and had broken bread, and eaten, and had talked with them a long while, even until break of day, he departed.
WEB.12: They brought the boy in alive, and were greatly comforted.
WEB.13: But we, going ahead to the ship, set sail for Assos, intending to take Paul aboard there; for he had so arranged, intending himself to go by land.
WEB.14: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard, and came to Mitylene.
WEB.15: Sailing from there, we came the following day opposite Chios. The next day we touched at Samos and stayed at Trogyllium, and the day after we came to Miletus.
WEB.16: For Paul had determined to sail past Ephesus, that he might not have to spend time in Asia; for he was hastening, if it were possible for him, to be in Jerusalem on the day of Pentecost.
WEB.17: From Miletus he sent to Ephesus, and called to himself the elders of the assembly.
WEB.18: When they had come to him, he said to them, “You yourselves know, from the first day that I set foot in Asia, how I was with you all the time,
WEB.19: serving the Lord with all humility, with many tears, and with trials which happened to me by the plots of the Jews;
WEB.20: how I didn’t shrink from declaring to you anything that was profitable, teaching you publicly and from house to house,
WEB.21: testifying both to Jews and to Greeks repentance toward God, and faith toward our Lord Jesus.
WEB.22: Now, behold, I go bound by the Spirit to Jerusalem, not knowing what will happen to me there;
WEB.23: except that the Holy Spirit testifies in every city, saying that bonds and afflictions wait for me.

WEB.25: “Now, behold, I know that you all, among whom I went about preaching God’s Kingdom, will see my face no more.
WEB.26: Therefore I testify to you today that I am clean from the blood of all men,
WEB.27: for I didn’t shrink from declaring to you the whole counsel of God.
WEB.28: Take heed, therefore, to yourselves, and to all the flock, in which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers, to shepherd the assembly of the Lord and  God which he purchased with his own blood.

WEB.34: You yourselves know that these hands served my necessities, and those who were with me.
WEB.35: In all things I gave you an example, that so laboring you ought to help the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that he himself said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’”
WEB.36: When he had spoken these things, he knelt down and prayed with them all.
WEB.37: They all wept a lot, and fell on Paul’s neck and kissed him,
WEB.38: sorrowing most of all because of the word which he had spoken, that they should see his face no more. And they accompanied him to the ship.

CSB17.1: After the uproar was over, Paul sent for the disciples, encouraged them, and after saying farewell, departed to go to Macedonia. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.2: And when he had passed through those areas and offered them many words of encouragement, he came to Greece
CSB17.3: and stayed three months. The Jews plotted against him when he was about to set sail for Syria, and so he decided to go back through Macedonia. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.4: He was accompanied <sup>[1]</sup> by Sopater son of Pyrrhus <sup>[2]</sup> from Berea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy, <sup>ⓛ</sup> and Tychicus <sup>ⓜ</sup> and Trophimus <sup>ⓝ</sup> from the province of Asia.
CSB17.5: These men went on ahead and waited for us in Troas, <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.6: but we sailed away from Philippi after the Festival of Unleavened Bread. <sup>ⓑ</sup> In five days we reached them at Troas, where we spent seven days.
CSB17.7: On the first day of the week, we <sup>[3]</sup> assembled to break bread. <sup>ⓒ</sup> Paul spoke to them, and since he was about to depart the next day, he kept on talking until midnight.
CSB17.8: There were many lamps in the room upstairs where we were assembled,
CSB17.9: and a young man named Eutychus was sitting on a window sill and sank into a deep sleep as Paul kept on talking. When he was overcome by sleep, he fell down from the third story and was picked up dead.
CSB17.10: But Paul went down, bent over him, embraced him, and said, “Don’t be alarmed, because he’s alive.” <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.11: After going upstairs, breaking the bread, and eating, Paul talked a long time until dawn. Then he left.
CSB17.12: They brought the boy home alive and were greatly comforted.
CSB17.13: We went on ahead to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we were going to take Paul on board, because these were his instructions, since he himself was going by land.
CSB17.14: When he met us at Assos, we took him on board and went on to Mitylene.
CSB17.15: Sailing from there, the next day we arrived off Chios. The following day we crossed over to Samos, and <sup>[4]</sup> the day after, we came to Miletus. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.16: For Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus to avoid spending time in the province of Asia, because he was hurrying to be in Jerusalem, if possible, for the day of Pentecost. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.17: Now from Miletus, he sent to Ephesus and summoned the elders of the church.
CSB17.18: When they came to him, he said to them: “You know, from the first day I set foot in Asia, how I was with you the whole time, <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.19: serving the Lord with all humility, with tears, and during the trials that came to me through the plots of the Jews. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.20: You know that I did not avoid proclaiming to you anything that was profitable or from teaching you publicly and from house to house.
CSB17.21: I testified to both Jews and Greeks about repentance toward God <sup>ⓘ</sup> and faith in our Lord Jesus. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.22: “And now I am on my way to Jerusalem, compelled by the Spirit, <sup>[5]</sup> not knowing what I will encounter there, <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.23: except that in every town the Holy Spirit warns me that chains and afflictions are waiting for me. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.24: But I consider my life of no value to myself; my purpose is to finish my course <sup>[6]</sup> and the ministry <sup>ⓜ</sup> I received from the Lord Jesus, <sup>ⓝ</sup> to testify to the gospel of God’s grace.
CSB17.25: “And now I know that none of you, among whom I went about preaching the kingdom, will ever see me again.
CSB17.26: Therefore I declare to you this day that I am innocent <sup>[7]</sup> of the blood of all of you, <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.27: because I did not avoid declaring to you the whole plan of God. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.28: Be on guard for yourselves and for all the flock <sup>ⓒ</sup> of which the Holy Spirit has appointed you as overseers, <sup>ⓓ</sup> to shepherd the church of God, <sup>[8]</sup> which he purchased with his own blood. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.29: I know that after my departure savage wolves will come in among you, not sparing the flock.
CSB17.30: Men will rise up even from your own number and distort the truth to lure the disciples into following them. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.31: Therefore be on the alert, remembering that night and day for three years I never stopped warning each one of you with tears. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.32: “And now <sup>[9]</sup> I commit you to God and to the word of his grace, <sup>ⓗ</sup> which is able to build you up and to give you an inheritance <sup>ⓘ</sup> among all who are sanctified.
CSB17.33: I have not coveted anyone’s silver or gold or clothing. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.34: You yourselves know that I worked with my own hands to support myself and those who are with me. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.35: In every way I’ve shown you that it is necessary to help the weak by laboring like this and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, because he said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’”
CSB17.36: After he said this, he knelt down and prayed with all of them. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.37: There were many tears shed by everyone. They embraced Paul and kissed him,
CSB17.38: grieving most of all over his statement that they would never see his face again. And they accompanied him to the ship. <sup>ⓜ</sup>

AMP.1: After the uproar had ended, Paul sent for the disciples, and when he had encouraged them he told them goodbye, and set off to go to Macedonia.
AMP.2: After he had gone through those districts and had encouraged the believers, he came to Greece.
AMP.3: And he stayed three months, and when a plot was formed against him by the Jews as he was about to set sail for Syria, he decided to return through Macedonia (northern Greece).
AMP.4: He was accompanied by Sopater of Berea, the son of Pyrrhus, and by Aristarchus and Secundus of the Thessalonians, and by Gaius of Derbe, and Timothy, and Tychicus and Trophimus of Asia.
AMP.5: These men went on ahead and were waiting for us (including Luke) at Troas.
AMP.6: We sailed from Philippi after the days of Unleavened Bread (Passover week), and within five days we reached them at Troas, where we stayed for seven days.
AMP.7: Now on the first day of the week (Sunday), when we were gathered together to break bread (share communion), Paul began talking with them, intending to leave the next day; and he kept on with his message until midnight.
AMP.8: Now there were many lamps in the upper room where we were assembled,
AMP.9: and there was a young man named Eutychus ("Lucky") sitting on the window sill. He was sinking into a deep sleep, and as Paul kept on talking longer and longer, he was completely overcome by sleep and fell down from the third story; and he was picked up dead.
AMP.10: But Paul went down and threw himself on him and embraced him, and said [to those standing around him], "Do not be troubled, because he is alive."
AMP.11: When Paul had gone back upstairs and had broken the bread and eaten, he talked [informally and confidentially] with them for a long time—until daybreak [in fact]—and then he left.
AMP.12: They took the boy [Eutychus] home alive, and were greatly comforted and encouraged.
AMP.13: But we went on ahead to the ship and set sail for Assos, intending to take Paul on board there; for that was what he had arranged, intending himself to go [a shorter route] by land.
AMP.14: So when he met us at Assos, we took him on board and sailed on to Mitylene.
AMP.15: Sailing from there, we arrived the next day [at a point] opposite Chios; the following day we crossed over to Samos, and the next day we arrived at Miletus [about 30 miles south of Ephesus].
AMP.16: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus so that he would not end up spending time [unnecessarily] in [the province of] Asia (modern Turkey); for he was in a hurry to be in Jerusalem, if possible, on the day of Pentecost.
AMP.17: However, from Miletus he sent word to Ephesus and summoned the elders of the church [to meet him there].
AMP.18:
And when they arrived he said to them:
"You know well how I [lived when I] was with you, from the first day that I set foot in Asia [until now],
AMP.19: serving the Lord with all humility and with tears and trials which came on me because of the plots of the Jews [against me];
AMP.20: [you know] how I did not shrink back in fear from telling you anything that was for your benefit, or from teaching you in public meetings, and from house to house,
AMP.21: solemnly [and wholeheartedly] testifying to both Jews and Greeks, urging them to turn in repentance to God and [to have] faith in our Lord Jesus Christ [for salvation].
AMP.22: And now, compelled by the Spirit and obligated by my convictions, I am going to Jerusalem, not knowing what will happen to me there,
AMP.23: except that the Holy Spirit solemnly [and emphatically] affirms to me in city after city that imprisonment and suffering await me.
AMP.24: But I do not consider my life as something of value or dear to me, so that I may [with joy] finish my course and the ministry which I received from the Lord Jesus, to testify faithfully of the good news of God's [precious, undeserved] grace [which makes us free of the guilt of sin and grants us eternal life].
AMP.25: "And now, listen carefully: I know that none of you, among whom I went about preaching the kingdom, will see me again.
AMP.26: For that reason I testify to you on this [our parting] day that I am innocent of the blood of all people.
AMP.27: For I did not shrink from declaring to you the whole purpose and plan of God.
AMP.28: Take care and be on guard for yourselves and for the whole flock over which the Holy Spirit has appointed you as overseers, to shepherd (tend, feed, guide) the church of God which He bought with His own blood.
AMP.29: I know that after I am gone, [false teachers like] ferocious wolves will come in among you, not sparing the flock;
AMP.30: even from among your own selves men will arise, speaking perverse and distorted things, to draw away the disciples after themselves [as their followers].
AMP.31: Therefore be continually alert, remembering that for three years, night or day, I did not stop admonishing and advising each one [of you] with tears.
AMP.32: And now I commend you to God [placing you in His protective, loving care] and [I commend you] to the word of His grace [the counsel and promises of His unmerited favor]. His grace is able to build you up and to give you the [rightful] inheritance among all those who are sanctified [that is, among those who are set apart for God's purpose—all believers].
AMP.33: I had no desire for anyone's silver or gold or [expensive] clothes.
AMP.34: You know personally that these hands ministered to my own needs [working in manual labor] and to [those of] the people who were with me.
AMP.35: In everything I showed you [by example] that by working hard in this way you must help the weak and remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that He Himself said, 'It is more blessed [and brings greater joy] to give than to receive.'"
AMP.36: When he had said these things, he knelt down and prayed with them all.
AMP.37: And they began to weep openly and threw their arms around Paul's neck, and repeatedly kissed him,
AMP.38: grieving and distressed especially over the word which he had spoken, that they would not see him again. And they accompanied him to the ship.

MSG.1: With things back to normal, Paul called the disciples together and encouraged them to keep up the good work in Ephesus. Then, saying his good-byes, he left for Macedonia.
MSG.2: Traveling through the country, passing from one gathering to another, he gave constant encouragement, lifting their spirits and charging them with fresh hope. Then he came to Greece
MSG.3: and stayed on for three months. Just as he was about to sail for Syria, the Jews cooked up a plot against him. So he went the other way, by land back through Macedonia, and gave them the slip.
MSG.4: His companions for the journey were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus, from Berea; Aristarchus and Secundus, both Thessalonians; Gaius from Derbe; Timothy; and the two from western Asia, Tychicus and Trophimus.
MSG.5: They went on ahead and waited for us in Troas.
MSG.6: Meanwhile, we stayed in Philippi for Passover Week, and then set sail. Within five days we were again in Troas and stayed a week.
MSG.7: We met on Sunday to worship and celebrate the Master's Supper. Paul addressed the congregation. Our plan was to leave first thing in the morning, but Paul talked on, way past midnight.
MSG.8: We were meeting in a well-lighted upper room.
MSG.9: A young man named Eutychus was sitting in an open window. As Paul went on and on, Eutychus fell sound asleep and toppled out the third-story window. When they picked him up, he was dead.

MSG.21: urging Jews and Greeks alike to a radical life-change before God and an equally radical trust in our Master Jesus.
MSG.22: "But there is another urgency before me now. I feel compelled to go to Jerusalem. I'm completely in the dark about what will happen when I get there.

MSG.25: "And so this is good-bye. You're not going to see me again, nor I you, you whom I have gone among for so long proclaiming the news of God's inaugurated kingdom.

MSG.28: "Now it's up to you. Be on your toes—both for yourselves and your congregation of sheep. The Holy Spirit has put you in charge of these people—God's people they are—to guard and protect them. God himself thought they were worth dying for.

MSG.30: from your very own ranks twisting words so as to seduce disciples into following them instead of Jesus.

NIV.1:
Through Macedonia and Greece
When the uproar had ended, Paul sent for the disciples and, after encouraging them, said good-by and set out for Macedonia.
NIV.2: He traveled through that area, speaking many words of encouragement to the people, and finally arrived in Greece,
NIV.3: where he stayed three months. Because the Jews made a plot against him just as he was about to sail for Syria, he decided to go back through Macedonia.
NIV.4: He was accompanied by Sopater son of Pyrrhus from Berea, Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica, Gaius from Derbe, Timothy also, and Tychicus and Trophimus from the province of Asia.
NIV.5: These men went on ahead and waited for us at Troas.
NIV.6: But we sailed from Philippi after the Feast of Unleavened Bread, and five days later joined the others at Troas, where we stayed seven days.
NIV.7:
Eutychus Raised From the Dead at Troas
On the first day of the week we came together to break bread. Paul spoke to the people and, because he intended to leave the next day, kept on talking until midnight.
NIV.8: There were many lamps in the upstairs room where we were meeting.
NIV.9: Seated in a window was a young man named Eutychus, who was sinking into a deep sleep as Paul talked on and on. When he was sound asleep, he fell to the ground from the third story and was picked up dead.
NIV.10: Paul went down, threw himself on the young man and put his arms around him. “Don’t be alarmed,” he said. “He’s alive!”
NIV.11: Then he went upstairs again and broke bread and ate. After talking until daylight, he left.
NIV.12: The people took the young man home alive and were greatly comforted.
NIV.13:
Paul’s Farewell to the Ephesian Elders
We went on ahead to the ship and sailed for Assos, where we were going to take Paul aboard. He had made this arrangement because he was going there on foot.
NIV.14: When he met us at Assos, we took him aboard and went on to Mitylene.
NIV.15: The next day we set sail from there and arrived off Kios. The day after that we crossed over to Samos, and on the following day arrived at Miletus.
NIV.16: Paul had decided to sail past Ephesus to avoid spending time in the province of Asia, for he was in a hurry to reach Jerusalem, if possible, by the day of Pentecost.
NIV.17: From Miletus, Paul sent to Ephesus for the elders of the church.
NIV.18: When they arrived, he said to them: “You know how I lived the whole time I was with you, from the first day I came into the province of Asia.
NIV.19: I served the Lord with great humility and with tears, although I was severely tested by the plots of the Jews.
NIV.20: You know that I have not hesitated to preach anything that would be helpful to you but have taught you publicly and from house to house.
NIV.21: I have declared to both Jews and Greeks that they must turn to God in repentance and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
NIV.22: “And now, compelled by the Spirit, I am going to Jerusalem, not knowing what will happen to me there.
NIV.23: I only know that in every city the Holy Spirit warns me that prison and hardships are facing me.
NIV.24: However, I consider my life worth nothing to me, if only I may finish the race and complete the task the Lord Jesus has given me—the task of testifying to the gospel of God’s grace.
NIV.25: “Now I know that none of you among whom I have gone about preaching the kingdom will ever see me again.
NIV.26: Therefore, I declare to you today that I am innocent of the blood of all men.
NIV.27: For I have not hesitated to proclaim to you the whole will of God.
NIV.28: Keep watch over yourselves and all the flock of which the Holy Spirit has made you overseers. Be shepherds of the church of God, which he bought with his own blood.
NIV.29: I know that after I leave, savage wolves will come in among you and will not spare the flock.
NIV.30: Even from your own number men will arise and distort the truth in order to draw away disciples after them.
NIV.31: So be on your guard! Remember that for three years I never stopped warning each of you night and day with tears.
NIV.32: “Now I commit you to God and to the word of his grace, which can build you up and give you an inheritance among all those who are sanctified.
NIV.33: I have not coveted anyone’s silver or gold or clothing.
NIV.34: You yourselves know that these hands of mine have supplied my own needs and the needs of my companions.
NIV.35: In everything I did, I showed you that by this kind of hard work we must help the weak, remembering the words the Lord Jesus himself said: ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’ ”
NIV.36: When he had said this, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
NIV.37: They all wept as they embraced him and kissed him.
NIV.38: What grieved them most was his statement that they would never see his face again. Then they accompanied him to the ship.
